_doc: MODEL CONFIG MANIFEST — the single versioned source for every model-semantics RL_*/PAR_* variable (gate-integrity repair, 2026-07-09, the durable half of R3). config_manifest.py loads THIS file; in BAKE/GATE mode it clears the ambient model environment, rejects unknown/divergent overrides, loads these values explicitly, and stamps config_sha256 into the produced artifacts' identity records (boot identity, book meta, gate report). Consumers verify code + store + CONFIG together. Ordinary dev-shell runs (no RL_CONFIG_MODE) are UNAFFECTED. Values are the env-string form the engine reads; they equal the current code defaults (post gate-integrity fix c, PAR_RAMPS default is 22), so clearing ambient + loading this manifest reproduces the board/book/panel byte-for-byte. Change a value ONLY at a bake, in the same commit that re-stamps config_sha256 and expected_boot.json.
baked_state: BAKED v2.7 (engine 7a07e369 / board e2c9bc51 / store a2fbc9a0)

excluded_infrastructure_vars._note: NOT model semantics — path/IO/display/mode plumbing, allowed ambient in bake/gate mode, never part of the value hash.
excluded_infrastructure_vars.RL_NO_OWNER_OVERRIDES: display-only Brodie override toggle (out of scope; never touches a guarded value)
excluded_infrastructure_vars.RL_ALLOW_PVCFIT_BOARD: R3 bake-write escape hatch (governed by the existing R3 bake guard + RULING-CONFIG)
excluded_infrastructure_vars.RL_VENV: pinned-venv path (setup_env.sh / SessionStart hook); not a model input
excluded_infrastructure_vars.PYTHONHASHSEED: determinism (not RL_/PAR_-prefixed; set by the pinned env)

var_notes.RL_RUCK_TAX: pinned for official-env parity; currently orphaned on the live board path (only verify_anchors.py, itself an orphan per docs/CHANGELOG, references rd.RUCK_TAX) — inert either way, pinned so bake/gate mode does not flag the official env's ambient 0.25 as unknown
var_notes.RL_PVCFIT: HELD OUT per owner ruling R3 (default 0); the R3 export bake-guard + RULING-CONFIG assertion remain the primary enforcement, this manifest reasserts 0 and rejects a divergent ambient 1 in bake/gate mode
var_notes.RL_LTI_CLOCK: advance = owner ruling R-i (2026-07-10, DECISIONS v90 §36): the L1c fade-clock ADVANCES during injury. The code default flips to advance in the same build; this manifest pins it so it rides artifact identity (config_sha256) and a bake/gate run with the clock PAUSED is rejected (divergent-override reject-scan + the ruling_config_check.py R-i assertion). Supersedes the provisional pause the #56 build shipped as default.
var_notes.PAR_RAMPS: 22 = official env; code default aligned 14->22 in gate-integrity fix c
